'''''UFC 262: Oliveira vs. Chandler''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on May 15, 2021, at the [[Toyota Center]] in [[Houston]], [[Texas]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.ufc.com/news/oliveira-vs-chandler-headline-ufc-262|title=Oliveira vs. Chandler to headline UFC 262|publisher=ufc.com|author=Staff|date=2021-03-19|access-date=2021-03-19}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/03/ufc-262-returns-texas-houston-toyota-center-host-event-full-crowd|title=UFC returns to Texas: Houston's Toyota Center to play host to UFC 262|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Mike Bohn|date=2021-03-19|access-date=2021-03-19}}</ref>

== Background ==
A [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] bout for the vacant title between former three-time [[List of Bellator MMA champions#Lightweight World Championship|Bellator Lightweight World Champion]] [[Michael Chandler]] and [[Charles Oliveira]] headlined the event.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.espn.com/mma/story/_/id/31090572/dana-white-khabib-nurmagomedov-officially-retired-michael-chandler-charles-oliveira-title|title=Dana White: Khabib Nurmagomedov officially retired; Michael Chandler-Charles Oliveira for title|work=espn.com|author=Brett Okamoto|date=2021-03-19|access-date=2021-03-19}}</ref> Former champion [[Khabib Nurmagomedov]] announced his retirement immediately after defending his title at [[UFC 254]] last October, citing his father's death from complications related to [[COVID-19]] in July as the main reason behind it.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2020/10/ufc-254-khabib-nurmagomedov-dominates-announces-retirement-ufc-254-justin-gaethje|title=Khabib Nurmagomedov dominates Justin Gaethje, retires after UFC 254 submission win|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Dave Doyle|date=2020-10-24|access-date=2021-03-19}}</ref> Despite his announcement, the title was never officially vacated as UFC President [[Dana White]] said several times that he believed Nurmagomedov would still fight again. White finally conceded on March 19 as he confirmed Nurmagomedov's retirement.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/3/19/22339456/dana-white-confirms-khabib-nurmagomedov-officially-retired-no-longer-pursuing-possible-return|title=Dana White confirms Khabib Nurmagomedov officially retired, no longer pursuing possible return|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2021-03-19|access-date=2021-03-19}}</ref>

A welterweight bout between [[Leon Edwards]] and former [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] challenger [[Nate Diaz]] (also [[The Ultimate Fighter 5]] lightweight winner) was expected to take place at this event. It would've marked the first five-round non-title co-main event in UFC history.<ref name="EdwardsDiaz">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/03/ufc-262-news-nate-diaz-vs-leon-edwards-five-rounds-co-main-event-houston|title=UFC 262 books Nate Diaz vs. Leon Edwards for five-round co-main event in Houston|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King and Mike Bohn|date=2021-03-30|access-date=2021-03-30}}</ref> However, Diaz pulled out due to a minor injury in early May and the bout was postponed to [[UFC 263]].<ref>{{Cite web|url= https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/5/3/22418618/leon-edwards-vs-nate-diaz-rescheduled-from-ufc-262-in-may-to-ufc-263-in-june|title= Leon Edwards vs. Nate Diaz rescheduled from UFC 262 in May to UFC 263 in June|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2021-05-03|access-date=2021-05-03}}</ref>

A flyweight bout between former [[UFC Flyweight Championship]] challenger [[Alex Perez (fighter)|Alex Perez]] and [[Matt Schnell]] was scheduled for the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://empiresportsmedia.com/mma/ufc-books-alex-perez-matt-schnell/|title=UFC books Alex Perez – Matt Schnell|publisher=empiresportsmedia.com|author=Daniel Cunningham|date=2021-02-22|access-date=2021-04-22}}</ref> However, Perez was forced to pull out due to undisclosed reasons. He was replaced by [[Rogério Bontorin]] and the bout took place at bantamweight.<ref name="Perez out">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/04/ufc-262-alex-perez-out-rogerio-bontorin-to-fight-matt-schnell|title=With Alex Perez out, Rogerio Bontorin targeted to fight Matt Schnell at UFC 262|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Farah Hannoun|date=2021-04-21|access-date=2021-04-22}}</ref> At the weigh-ins, Bontorin weighed in at 137 pounds, one pound over the division's non-title fight limit. The bout proceeded at catchweight and he was fined 20% of his purse, which went to Schnell.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/05/ufc-262-charles-oliveira-michael-chandler-weigh-in-results-live|title=UFC 262 weigh-in results: Charles Oliveira, Michael Chandler set for vacant title|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2021-05-14|access-date=2021-05-14}}</ref>

[[Joel Álvarez]] was scheduled to face [[Christos Giagos]] in a lightweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmadna.nl/joel-alvarez-treft-christos-giagos-tijdens-ufc-262/|title=Joel Alvarez faces Christos Giagos at UFC 262|publisher=mmadna.nl|author=Staff|date=2021-03-12|access-date=2021-05-06}} {{in lang|nl}}</ref> However, Álvarez was removed from the bout in early May due to alleged [[visa (document)|visa]] issues that restricted his travel. Giagos faced returning veteran [[Sean Soriano]] instead.<ref name="Álvarez out">{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/05/ufc-262-sean-soriano-returns-after-six-years-vs-christos-giagos|title=Sean Soriano returns to UFC following six-year absence, faces Christos Giagos at UFC 262|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=John Morgan and Nolan King|date=2021-05-06|access-date=2021-05-06}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between [[Jack Hermansson]] and [[Edmen Shahbazyan]] was expected to take place at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/3/20/22341466/jack-hermansson-vs-edmen-shahbazyan-booked-for-ufc-262|title=Jack Hermansson vs. Edmen Shahbazyan booked for UFC 262|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2021-03-20|access-date=2021-05-10}}</ref> However, the bout was postponed to take place one week later at [[UFC Fight Night: Font vs. Garbrandt]] due to a COVID-19 case in Hermansson's camp.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/05/ufc-262-news-jack-hermansson-vs-edmen-shabazyan-shifts-fight-night-188|title=UFC 262 loses Jack Hermansson vs. Edmen Shahbazyan; bout pushed back one week|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2021-05-10|access-date=2021-05-10}}</ref>

On May 14, it was announced that the UFC would be increasing bonuses (Fight of the Night and Performance of the Night) from $50,000 to $75,000. This increase was for this event only and was made at the request of former interim lightweight champion [[Tony Ferguson]] (also [[The Ultimate Fighter: Team Lesnar vs. Team dos Santos]] welterweight winner), who received a raucous response from the audience in attendance at the UFC 262 pre-fight press conference when he made the proposal.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2021/05/ufc-262-dana-white-raises-bonus-money-75k|title=Dana White obliges Tony Ferguson, increases UFC 262 bonuses to $75,000|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=John Morgan and Nolan King|date=2021-05-14|access-date=2021-05-15}}</ref>

Several hooligans fought in the stands during this event.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.sportskeeda.com/mma/news-watch-fans-go-crazy-fight-breaks-stands-ufc-262 | title=Watch: Fans go crazy as a fight breaks out in the stands at UFC 262 | date=16 May 2021 }}</ref>

==Bonus awards==
The following fighters received $75,000 bonuses.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/5/16/22438435/ufc-262-bonuses-new-champ-charles-oliveira-banks-75k-for-michael-chandler-knockout|title=UFC 262 bonuses: New champ Charles Oliveira banks $75k for Michael Chandler knockout|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Steven Marrocco|date=2021-05-16|access-date=2021-05-16}}</ref>
* '''Fight of the Night: Edson Barboza vs. Shane Burgos'''
* '''Performance of the Night: Charles Oliveira''' and ''' Christos Giagos'''

==Aftermath==
In June 2021, Bontorin was notified by [[USADA]] due to a potential doping violation. He tested positive for [[hydrochlorothiazide]], a banned diuretic, in an out-of-competition urine sample in May 2021.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/6/15/22534696/ufc-flyweight-rogerio-bontorin-tests-positive-diuretic-mma|title=UFC flyweight Rogerio Bontorin tests positive for diuretic|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2021-06-15|access-date=2021-06-16}}</ref> Following notification of his positive test, Bontorin provided open containers of two dietary supplements he obtained from a Brazilian compounding pharmacy that he was using prior to his positive test for analysis by a WADA-accredited laboratory. Although no prohibited substances were listed on the supplement product labels, the analysis revealed the presence of HCTZ in the products. He was given a three-month suspension that began on 1 May and his win was overturned to a no contest.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2021/10/1/22703949/rogerio-bontorin-reduced-usada-suspension-tainted-supplement-brandon-royval-january-15-ufc|title=Rogerio Bontorin gets reduced suspension for tainted supplement, returns vs. Brandon Royval at Jan. 15 UFC event|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz and Mike Heck|date=2021-10-01|access-date=2022-05-02}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.ufc.com/news/rogerio-bontorin-learned-the-right-lessons-ufc-fight-night-kattar-chikadze-flyweight|title=Rogério Bontorin learned the right lessons|publisher=ufc.com|author=E. Spencer Kyte|date=2022-01-12|access-date=2022-05-02}}</ref>
